channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 147 replies — 588.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 147 • Updated: 2026-06-24 11:04 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

62395589
>>62394870
Don’t you need like 7 figs worth of pulsechain to be a validator

62396733
>>62377801
Why would you want those with over 10K to leave? It sounds like you could use some advice especially if you're five figures and "investing" in trading cards. Your finest minds have more than 10K because they minimally are employed with decent jobs.

62398741
>>62395577
I agree with your thesis that we should accumulate up and coming software players with great ai

that said

> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet

we're too early

> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)

https://www.azfamily.com/2026/01/08/waymo-passenger-flees-after-car-drives-phoenix-light-rail-tracks/

I live in AZ, everyone hates Waymo. Uber drivers will literally bully the crap out of them


>Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)

Too broad

>Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)

This has to be inevitable by the end of the next decade. The writing is on the wall, I mean think of Siri and Alexa, different in scope but has easily prepared consumers to accept the eventuality of "you" just running your own tasks. Small business owners would also love this. Imagine a traveler who stays at an Airbnb month long for business and uploads his AI to manage the boring stuff of his new "home" while he acclimates to the business and travel of the area.


>medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery

this has potential

>mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

We're too early

62398767
>>62398741
https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1132599

>Publishing in the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) online June 22, the study team found that online portal messages more than doubled between 2020 and 2025 (by 153 percent). By contrast, total telephone calls decreased by 6 percent over the same period.

>“Clinical staff will need to be trained in mastering the tools of messaging in healthcare; in using AI support programs, including chatbots that can frame content to minimize its complexity; and in making the most effective use of clinician time needed for online billing and online counseling,” said Dr. Segev, who is also a profession in NYU Grossman’s Department of Population Health.

>Already, he noted, NYU Langone uses AI support tools to speed up drafting of physician and provider notes.


Doctors already use google to diagnose people

62398789
Some topical reading for today, gentleman, nothing too difficult takes less than 5 mins. Lets take this slow and not bite off more than we can chew

62398798
>>62396733
They'd have no reason to frequent and appreciate these threads or this company. You think rich boomers understand our circumstances?

62398799
Is diversification a meme? Thinking it's better to just go all in on a single stock than an index fund when you have a small account.

62398806
Page 2, come on anon, it doesn't hurt to learn the basics, i'll go over with you, together

62398809
>>62398799
How much are you putting down? I'm thinking of doing $1000 all in picks and making a folder that way

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62398985
>>62376699
> you're arguing with a script and losing

62399017
>>62380691
bump

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

item.title: VOO and Chill — 36 replies — 144.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-24 11:02 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400636
Thing is, index funds are for either:
A) People who have a lot of money where % changes magnify in a meaningful way
B) People who start early enough in life and consistently enough for compounding to mean anything

The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.

>b-but something is better than nothing!
Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....

62400638
>>62400633
You can that with VOO and chill though…

62400645
>>62400638
>5% withdrawal rate

YIKES
DESU you need 5mil, and you aren't getting there without constantly invest invest invest and wa

62400651
>>62400645
*waiting and hoping you don't die along the way.

62400652
>>62400554
ngmi

62400699
>>62400652
Exactly you can’t explain it
>>62400636
Well I’m 26 and have low expenses so I’ve been DCA my salary into VOO
I guess at 26 I could have more risk but idk this is hitting 20% returns after a year and a half good enough for me

62400995
Qqq is just more narrow and carries more risk. It’s true that s&p and nasdaq carry many of the same companies right now, but nasdaq can crash and fail to recover, or growth can move to another segment of the economy. S&P will adjust when either of these happen. Nasdaq will be Nasdaq. It seems if you invest in both that’s fine, but you are putting your money in the same place twice and increasing risk.

62401051
>>62400995
From what I’ve seen QQQ has basically just mirrored VOO with a factor of 2
Twice the gains, twice the loses and actually quite a few years where the two were equal
I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?

62401312
What a pointless thread. Oh wow have you come to the conclusion you should just buy an ETF wow what a waste of space this shit thread is.

62401466
>>62401312
Sorry I should’ve made a thread where I pretend to buy a stock and ask what I’m in for instead
Or posting a red angry basedjak

62401489
>>62401312
ngmi

62401790
>>62400620
Putting in 100 bucks per month makes you average.

62402005
>>62400493
VWCE and chill

62402931
>>62401790
Any image with a wojak means you should follow the opposite of whatever advice it is advocating
Additionally
>100 bucks a month
LMAO ARE YOU POOR

62402981
ETFs only make sense if you're already rich and looking for a source of passive income

62402983
>>62402981
no they don't lmao

62402988
>>62402981
>going for that passive income

62402991
>>62402983
Yeah he's right if you have enough capital you can easily live off it. The problem is morons with 10k thinking VOO is going to make them rich after investing for a few years.

62402995
>>62402991
no he isn't idiot

62403030
>>62402931
The average person is pretty poor. And this is just braindead fire and forget index fund investing. 100 or 120 per month gives you 200k in 40 years.
You can put in 500 or 600 per month if you want a million in 40 years. Of course it won't be a million, it will be more like 5 million or 10 million due to hyper inflation, but it will be equal to what a million is today.

62403034
>>62402981
ETFs are how you make generational wealth without thinking. It literally requires no work. Picking stocks burns brainpower and also has a high failure rate.

62403044
>>62402991
lmao no, etfs are good for retirement as a middle class wagecuck; they wont make you rich sure but to claim is only useful for millionaires is bullshit.
Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this.

62403053
>>62403044
The median 64 year old american has 400k to retire on and they seem to be ok with it.
That's what middle class means in america. Of course 1 in 10 Americans have a net worth of 1 million, but that's not controlled for age.

62403140
>>62400636
Im the second one.
Yeehaw

62403184
>>62403140
same

62403215
>>62403030
>The average person is pretty poor.

Kind of.

>The U.S. median household income is $83,730. For individual personal income, the median figure is $45,140. -- St. Louis FED

Most Americans can afford to save more than $100/month, especially if they are married or have a SO, but most Americans really don't think about the future, so they finance the best lifestyle the bank is willing to finance instead of living well beneath their means. Most Americans should not be going to Disneyworld or on vacations every year, especially on credit, but they do and this leads them to get into debt, making it "impossible" to save money.

I try to save as much as possible because no way am I not going to settle for $200k in the year of our Lord 2060. That's broke money today, much less in 40 years when the USD is further in the toilet.

62404485
>>62400560
I want to be free. The colour of my bugatti is meat colored

62404617
>>62400493
it's over

62404649
>>62400515
And jewish

62404732
>>62400493
Im all in on aussie etf covering nasdaq NDQ, 60k dollars so far. But will probably start getting other stuff to be safe. I figured its fine if it goes down 50% at this early stage cause 30k isnt world ending

item.title: Irl crime — 61 replies — 132.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-06-24 10:43 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62397200
>>62393307
When I was a young teenager, I had a friend who bought a bb gun pistol. He then waited for evening, went to the park, hid behind the bushes near a path, and then jumped out at old people walking and stole their wallets and jewelry. He made a lot of money. He asked me to join in, but I wasn't interested.

No idea what happened to him. Probably dead. Good riddance.

62397518
Bump

62397557
>>62394022
What a literal fucking nigger. Repent

62397565
>>62396900
Retards posting obviously fake quotes is always funny

62397959
>>62397557
Kys

62398384
>>62393307
Me on the picrel

62398550
>>62393307
>>62393356
>>62394022
>>62395812
>>62397020
>>62397565
>>62397959
Izzat farmers
Abandon thread

62398943
Bump

62399147
>>62393307
Sleep Tight Pigger

62399469
This one time my bros and i knew about 35 mil worth of gold bars so we painted the floor beneath their safe with explosives and then had a decoy boat drive off as we cracked the safe in the water below. Then we scuba'd the bars to a seperate boat, and wbile celebrating one of our own bros decided to double cross us as we celebrated our heist. Fuckin loser didnt even know what to do with all that money besides copy all our ideas. So anyway we come up with a plan to steal the gold back by sending our dead safe cracker's daughter (whom the coward was unfamiliar with) to sneak into his house disguised as a television internet repair woman, and we cut the lines of his tv. Our plans were almost ruined when his neighbors had a house party so we called it off and had to rethink our plans; we surveilled him and found out he was moving our gold in 3 trucks so we modded 3 mini coopers all souped up and pulled another demolished floor trick, the loser viewing from the helicopter couldnt see because we had a billboard truck that was blocking the view of the gold truck falling into the subway below. We knew which truck the gold was in because the ride height and our hackerbro friend basically led them right into a traffic jam above the explosive floor. After incapacitating the drivers we cracked the safe and transported the gold in our minis to a train. Our former betrayor tried to buy his way onto the train only to find that we paid off the mobsters that owned the train a ton of cash and gold to be part of our plan too. Then we listened to money by pink floyd.

62399612
>>62399469

That hackerbro's name? Albert Einstein.

62399967
>>62393322
I one time worked at Walmart a summer in high school and someone was cutting up bags to eat handfuls and I had to clean it. They didn’t give me a lunch break because I was so new and I was throwing away dozens of bags so I ate a single chip in a bag that was popped but not fully opened. Got fired on the spot as they were watching me on video like a hawk. Fuck that gay company. I’ve never bought from them since

62400055
>>62399967
Lol did you learn your retard-goy lesson?

62400082
>>62393319
Bestiality is technically illegal

62400093
I have not and will commit any crime as I am a model citizen and do not break the law.
Anything I have posted on this site that contradicts that is fairy tales and only a fool would take those such posts as fact.

62400095
>>62400093
Will NOT commit*
God fucking damn

62400096
>>62400093
Wow that's really clever and original and funny

62400920
>>62393307
comfy

62400948
>>62393307
You call that a crime scene?
Here’s a real big one bed in the street you lightweight!

62401887
Bump

62401956
This is the most pathetic fed agent trap Ive ever seen.
You guys, speaking to LE here, realize that most people on 4chan are just regular people? Maybe a little edgier and weirder? Go fuck yourself.

62401967
>>62393307
I stole some dinosaur toys from my younger cousin. My aunt and uncle obviously noticed but let it slip because they knew I was a poor kid.

Also, I guess picking up banknotes after old people drop them once they get up from public transportation seating? I have "stolen" like 20 bucks in my life that way. Why do the old and disabled retards don't give a shit about losing money, anyways?

62401994
>>62401956
>Listen here kid, you HAVE to follow arbitrary Jewish law!

62401997
I worked at my Mom's job for a Boomercare home when Covid first broke out. There was a massive shortage of nurses so a good amount of the tasks were definitely illegal since I was just some guy in zoom college and didn't have any qualifications to be anywhere near any of the patients, but it was very comfy and ironically helped me get the job I work now more than that college diploma did lol

62402026
>>62393307
>Dusty summer sky
>Eucalyptus
>Wild boar
>yellow plates
>Commie block
>Paper only recycling bins
Israel, My guess is within 250meters of downtown tel aviv
Watch yourself Lior, you are glowing.

62402123
>>62393424
The only thing he was right about tho was buying bitcoin early. You don’t need to do crime for that.

62402179
>>62401956
Yeah I'm an NSA agent trying to catch criminals using this thread.

>>62402026
Maybe the Hebrew writing would've been the clue to follow here, Detective Dipshit.
On no, looks like you've uncovered my glowie operation for Tel Aviv!


Jews are absolutely right about goyim and niggers are right about hickeys and jeets are tight about timmies.
There's a very good reason for your current predicament

62402182
>>62402179
Honkeys*
Whitoids

62403110
>62402179
Fag

62404699
Bump

item.title: Binance out of the EU? — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Binance out of the EU?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-24 11:03 UTC

OP

How is this going to affect the crypto market?

Early replies (first 3)

62404563
>>62404556
>the crypto market
what?

you thought theres still a market?

lol

why you think every pump gets sold off?

underwater MMs are slowly offloading to $0 into ever-decreasing demand thats your market

62404564
>>62404556
why are you still talking about crypto even? Everyone with a brain has moved onto stocks

62404566
>>62404563
MMs? Market makers? Dont those cunts make their money out of the spread?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404567
>>62404564
I still have a bunch of usdt and there are things like staking, gold stablecoins and spacex gambling.

62404608
>>62404556
peepoo ll hold their own coins and slimy cz wont be able to manipulate the prices so easily.

62404611
>>62404556
see how dast the lil droids sliding the thred, it fears :D

62404615
>>62404556
Anyone here still using binance?

62404622
>>62404556
What kind of idiot uses Binance?

62404679
>>62404622
It seems that there are a lot of idiots anon, is the biggest exchange in the world.

62404688
>>62404622
Whats the better alternative? And please dont tell mot robinhood or some shit

62404733
>>62404556
That could cause a dump

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 35 replies — 28.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-24 09:57 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398057
>>62395915
Because I can hold it for the long term while exploring new ways to put it to work.

62398163
>>62398057
Might as well do a bank transfer as it's more private, costs less and the money won't disappear into thin air.

62398819
>>62395655
Because it's not actual Bitcoin, unlike Bitcoin SV (ticker: BSV), which I'm holding.

62399676
>>62398819
BSV is super dead super super dead

62399898
>>62395655
BTC is dead.
Western governments are all over it, they want your Capital Gains via taxes.
Unless you got in early and got out of the country with your gains early you’re fucked. Australia in its recent tax changes has hidden a trap for crypto gains, by stating any earnings made overseas in the last 12 years must be retroactively given to the government to be taxed, if you’re audited and your physical wealth exceeds your earnings, you’re fucked.
Thats outside that statute of limitations for records. It’s a dual purpose instrument- to catch people working in tax havens like Dubai who claimed they were leaving Australia forever then returned 10 years later with unexplained millions, but also to catch crypto investors who were using decentralised exchanges. The ATO has been using bots to crawl through ledgers linking transactions to addresses and piecing together expenditure and entry. If you’re caught it’s not avoidance it’s evasion.

It’s too late now but y’all should’ve moved over to XMR years ago.

62400285
>>62399898
Btc is dead? Wow. Who are you?

62400293
>>62398057
>>62398163
How!!!???

62400366
>>62395655
Worst opportunity by every metric.

62400842
>>62396879
Why do people go to the casino?

62400859
>>62400285
Epstein, Jeffrey Epstein.

62400895
>>62395655
microstrategies fucked it up. he's so maxdebt and overleveraged it's too easy to rip his company apart with the next recession and then take his btc for pennies on the dollar

62400974
>>62400895
also notice the ceo for mstr is white male. we know the results already

62401175
>>62400974
Is he gay? The way he speaks

62401180
>>62400859
Where are you hiding boss? I know you're alive

62401190
>>62400293
Oh no, stop staking your btc

62401192
I hold 3.4btc. is it enough?

62401205
>>62395655
Token not needed.

62401259
>>62395655
My IQ is >85

62402158
>>62395925
What's the tax rate there?

62402616
2x pump potential from here AT MOST, while the downside potential (probably $30k bottom) is terrible.

62402679
>>62402158

62403094

62404024

item.title: >wait for dip >use entire portfolio to buy in the money options for SPXL >buy options with a exercise date 2-3 years out… — 21 replies — 26.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>wait for dip >use entire portfolio to buy in the money options for SPXL >buy options with a exercise date 2-3 years out…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-24 10:24 UTC

OP

>wait for dip
>use entire portfolio to buy in the money options for SPXL
>buy options with a exercise date 2-3 years out
>wait
>outperform the SP 500 by 10x

What am I missing here? It seems like this would be how the average wagie could retire in 2-3 years time.

Early replies (first 3)

62402192
won't taxes fuck you over?

62402211
>>62402039
today was your perfect opportunity.

>>62402192
stop posting here dumbass.

62402400
>>62402192
Yeah thats why my goal each year is to make 0 dollars. Thats how I make sure I never have to pay taxes on my gains.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62402508
>Wait for the dip

Do you have a crystal ball telling you when the dip will happen?

62402546
>>62402508
If you're watching it every day, you can tell.

62402593
>>62402039
>wait
>>outperform the SP 500 by 10x
You sure about that?

62402612
>>62402039
This real? I got 6m, I now want to try this 100k experiment.

62402689
>>62402508
>market drops 20% over a few months
I meant waiting for a bear market which will happen

62402698
>>62402593
SPXL not spy

62402699
>>62402593
you used SPY options not SPXL

62402707
>>62402039
No guarantee SPX won't go down further and stay down for 10 years

62402712
>>62402508
i guessed the last 3 bottoms exactly within like 2-3 days precision

62402724
>>62402039
leveraged etfs are only good for a short term position. i forget whats it called, but they will bleed you out by virtue of how they have to manage themselves. in almost every instance its better to use real leverage than it is to use an etf

62402732
>>62402724
leverage decay but also that doesn't matter if it keeps going up

62402748
>>62402508
it's happening right now

62402754
>>62402732
>>62402724
Forced deleveraging.

62402755
>>62402724
This gets repeated ad nauseum and when you point out how tqqq went from 40 cents to $85 over the long term you get a ramble about luck like the post 1880 pattern isn't up.

62404200
>>62402039
1
Wait for Volatility to Terminally Spike
Monitoring VIX
Do not buy on the first or second red days. Wait until the broader market volatility index (VIX) stretches past 35–40. This signals true retail capitulation, which is usually when a 30% index drop finds its structural floor.
2
Identify the New TQQQ Baseline
Post-Crash Baseline
Locate where TQQQ stabilizes. If it dropped from $80 down to $20 during the index's 30% drop, $20 is your new baseline strike target.
3
Select an Expiration Date 1.5 Years Out
Buying Time
Look at the options chain and select LEAPs expiring at least 12 to 18 months into the future. This ensures that even if the market spends 3 months grinding sideways at the bottom, your position won't bleed out from time decay.
4
Purchase the At-The-Money (ATM) Strike
Position Sizing
Buy the strike price closest to the current spot price (e.g., the $20 Call). Because you are buying deep value with high intrinsic probability, a move back to just $45 on TQQQ (a normal cyclical bounce) can easily expand that option premium by 1,000% as it goes deep into the money.

62404249
>>62402039
You are not missing anything. The average normgroid simply doesn’t know that this is an option or consider it “too risky”. The latter is such a retarded, self contradictory point I won’t even honor it with an explanation.

62404661
>>62402039
The only thing you're missing is that it requires no time sensitivity. If you have a date (e.g. you want to retire at 40 and you are currently 30) then you can wake up on your 40th birthday and be in the negatives when a year before you were 10x up.
So long as you're willing to push back such plans by up to 25 years, though, you will always profit bigly from this strategy.

62404666
>>62402724
That's bullshit and has been extensively studied even in the scientific literature.

item.title: Polkadot update — 26 replies — 18.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-24 09:46 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084

62401689
>>62401678

62401700

62401739
>>62401689

Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877

62401946

62402307
>>62399251

62402419

item.title: USA vs Germany drug pricing conflict — 35 replies — 18.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
USA vs Germany drug pricing conflict

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-24 09:19 UTC

OP

>The United States has launched a trade investigation into Germany
over its plans to cut spending on pharmaceuticals.
>U.S. Trade Representative Jameson Greer says the probe is looking into
whether Berlin's drug pricing policies are unfair or discriminatory towards American companies.
>The investigation comes under Section 301 of the U.S. Trade Act
and could lead to tariffs on German exports if Washington finds evidence of unfair practice.
>Germany is facing growing financial pressure on its public health care system
with a projected budget shortfall of around €20 billion.
>To help control the cost, Berlin has proposed measures that would require pharmaceutical companies
to offer larger discounts on expensive new drugs.
>However, the Trump administration argues that such policies allow countries like Germany
to access medicines at much lower prices,
>forcing American consumers to shoulder a disproportionate share of the cost of developing new medicines.
Just two months ago, Washington used these exact same trade threats
>to pressure the UK into a historic deal,
forcing the British government to effectively double its spending on new medicines
>to avoid American tariffs.

https://www.dailymotion.com/video/xah8e3m

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/19/us-tariff-probe-germany-drug-pricing-policies-mfn.html

https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/us-launches-section-301-probe-into-germany-over-drug-pricing-2026-06-19/

https://www.euronews.com/business/2026/06/19/us-launches-trade-probe-into-germany-over-medicine-prices

Early replies (first 3)

62404118
>>62404112
pretty sure the SCOTUS ruled most of trump's tariffs illegal

also pretty sure germany knows this

62404127
>>62404118
>Just two months ago, Washington used these exact same trade threats
>to pressure the UK into a historic deal,
>forcing the British government to effectively double its spending on new medicines
>to avoid American tariffs.

62404280
>>62404127
But cant Germany rely on the EU therefore saving itself?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404311
Boehringer Ingelheim is scaling back planned R&D investments in Germany in response to the proposed German legislation. I find this strange since it's a German company. I thought this was about American pharmaceutical companies.

62404320
I posted some stuff in these threads. Hopefully someone finds the parallel I'm drawing to the fish and wine conflict interesting, which AI agreed with and gave a good elaboration on, see picrel and these threads.

>>>/news/1520712

>>>/pol/537508422

I think this is about the state using business as a power tool, just like The East India Company and The United Fruit Company. Hopefully we can discuss that.

62404327
>>62404112
Why do you post like a fuckin sped?

62404333
>>62404320
I cropped out the irrelevant parts.

62404338
>>62404112
american pharma shareholder jew wants more shekels

62404343
>>62404112
good, yuropoors are gonna pay up

62404346
>>62404338
>>62404343

62404364
>>62404333
>>62404346
Ok but how is this 100 year old paper relevant?

62404366
>>62404118
Trump can just use military aid to Ukraine as leverage to force the EU countries to do anything he wants

62404369
>>62404112
Kek i have this feeling that soon the USA will start ignoring patents and allow vsrious things to be copied without being able to get sued by foreign companies

62404383
>yuropoors think they're allowed to lower the price of their medicine
LOL

62404425
>>62404364
>Frito Pendejo stare

62404430
>>62404383
I was hoping for actual discussion.

62404440
>>62404112
The government is a client just like any other, but because it's a bigger client than a bunch of shitty hospitals they have more negotiation power and get better deals. I find it amusing braindead drumbers who run their mouth about free markets and business all day can't understand something so basic.

62404448
>>62404320
It's about the american government acting retarded. Using tariffs to damage strategic competitors like china would be a good use of them. But if you really are concerned about trade deficits, as they claim they are, all you need to do is stop deficit spending and pretending all that foreign money you're borrowing goes nowhere.
In this particular case they have this idea that pharma companies charge americans more because europoors pay less, and not because their prices are negotiated by weak fragmented players that take kickbacks. If prices in europe go up, the profit margins of the pharma companies go up, and americans keep paying the same because they have a lump of fat in the place where you'd expect to find their brain.

62404459
>>62404440
Yeah but the US government is threatening with tariffs on German goods if they put a lower price. Shoving the medicines down their throat.
>No you can't have these drugs for $10, pay $20 or we'll buy fewer of your BMWs.
As I said it's exactly like the fish and wine conflict.
>we don't want to buy your wine
>then we won't buy your fish
>ok, just kidding, we want to buy your wine

62404462
>>62404455
I reckon it's more about a bunch of poor and starving pharma executives showing up in the white house (or the office of one of the illiterate clowns he has for ministers) and crying that the evil german government was trying to take their lunch money, and because drumber is such a charitable soul and hates the german chancelor because he called him a pathetic loser that got beaten up by a bunch of ragheads in iran, he decided to take up their cause, dog bless his soul

62404464
>>62404448
They say the motive is that Americans pay more for drugs, yes. But I think that might just be rhetoric to get the American populace to feel that it's right. I think it goes deeper. What politicians say they're doing and what they're actually doing are rarely the same thing.

62404468
>>62404464
refer to>>62404462
they got lobbied to cuck germany, it really is that simple with these people

62404470
>>62404280
lmao

62404480
>>62404468
I think there was lobbying yes but I think these things are a two-way street. Copying a post I posted in the /pol/ thread:

this is about the state using corporations as a power tool. Same thing that happened with The East India Company and The United Fruit Company. Business (corporations) is the engine of the state. I think the alcohol situation I mentioned a hundred years ago is a perfect analogy for what's going on. Imagine a country has a lot of vineyards. One country is trying to ban alcohol. The first country imposes tariffs in retaliation. Then the wine begins flowing again. The wine export brings money into the state, even if the vineyards are privately owned. This is how corporations and the state are two sides of the same coin. The state uses the vineyard as a power tool, the vineyard uses the state to get the wine sold, a two-way street. That's the same thing with this situation. Germany wants to keep drug prices low, like Norway wanted to limit alcohol consumption. The exporting country wants to increase their own wealth and power. Same dynamic.

62404484
Are we finally learning that 'free healthcare' isn't really free?

62404496
>>62404484
in my country we get taxed heavily, to pay for 'free' health care, but it wasn't enough, so the government introduced a 'temporary' levy in 1984 (now 2% of your income) to pay for said 'free' health care and then this still wasn't enough so a 'temporary' surcharge was introduced in 1997, additional to the 'temporary' levy, for those who could afford private health insurance but did not have it.

62404504
>>62404112
who the fuck cares what germans have to say

62404509
>>62404496
oh I forgot to mention that the premiums for private health care are greater than the 'temporary' surcharge so people are opting to pay it instead of getting private health insurance and now the entire insurance industry is at risk of collapsing due to the 5.5 million boomers (21.5% of the total population) all starting to require increasing health care at the same time and not enough people paying into the private health system to cover the ever increasing costs.

62404520
>>62404366
He has already dumped the costs of supporting Ukraine onto European countries. There is virtually nothing more to cut, unless he wants to cut C4ISR support and thus throw the war. Despite the impression some people get from his public rhetoric, he doesn't want to do that.

62404533
>>62404468
>>62404464
>>62404480

It's not just corporations. It's the entirety of the pharma industry. You have
-Private hospitals
-Health insurance companies
-Pharmaceutical companies
They're all working together to squeeze the average US citizen for as much cash as possible. That's why you have insane prices for a single bandaid in a hospital.

By lobbying and pushing for higher prices on pharmaceuticals in Germany or the entire EU, the government won't be able to keep up the free healthcare thing (which is already unsustainable btw) and force people to resort to private healthcare (insurances/hospitals which is already somewhat a thing in EU). Once that happens it will facilitate
-More private healthcare/insurance companies
-Higher cost of treatments
-Bigger profit margins for pharma companies
It's not just about allowing some pharma companies to charge more, it's about trying to spread the infection that is the private healthcare system in the US to the EU, a largely untapped market. It's painfully obvious.

62404539
>we dont have money
>Lets force them to sell it for cheaper
Its that easy huh?

62404552
>>62404533
>muh one-way street
Didn't read because you didn't read jack shit of my posts.

62404562
>>62404484
More like, the free market is bullshit, and has always been.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 147 replies — 18.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 147 • Updated: 2026-06-24 03:21 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398220
>>62397819
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Decentralized does not have to mean the cypherpunk fantasy of "banks aren't needed and everyone is a sovereign individual." It's largely a matter of semantics.
Decentralized simply means that the nodes participating in the network are independent of one another.
If I tell you to watch a football game and report the result back to me in exchange for money, it's difficult to guarantee that you're telling the truth.
If I tell you and another person—whom you don't know—to watch the same game and report the result back to me, my chances of learning the true result increase, because the two of you would have to find each other, coordinate, and risk not getting paid.
Now imagine I tell 20 people to do the same, and on top of that, each person must deposit $1,000 to be eligible to participate and earn money. Nobody has an incentive to lie, because doing so would put their deposit, any future payments for their service, and their reputation as football-game reporters—all of which generate income for them—at risk. That's a decentralized oracle network.
This is the meaning of decentralization and what Chainlink is building - consensus without a chain. Consensus as a service.
I'm done spoon feeding for today. Hope that was clear and helpful to all the perplexed and confused anons here.

62398292
>>62398220
Is McDonald's decentralized because they have 5 employees that I can ask what the price of a big Mac is?

62398491
>>62396382
"token holders don't get any revenue (pause for the clips...) at this point"
>at this point
it's a trick.

62400097
>>62397938

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400134
>>62398726
it seems like only china understands the importance of space and musk has spent 20 yrs trying to drill this into the thick heads of amerigolem goys but they never listen, same thing with evs, they never took off because of the vehicle cartels, then you hear leftist faggots say he never innovated when he was up against goy cattle and their stubborn ways of thinking, now look at what they are doing, desperately trying to get a piece because china woke them up

62400164
>>62398018
I bought GME at $64. It's a small membership fee to be in the perpetual baggie club. Welcome. Think I should join the SPCX community as well. Nice people. Waiting for $200 to buy.

62400173
>>62398726
>nobody is launching and landing rockets
fixed that for you

62400218
>>62398726
>nobody else is launching and landing rockets like SpaceX
What for? To spy on everyone on behalf of the US military? How does that produces anything or make any money?

62401728
>>62397993
Every single IPO I've ever seen the price nosedives until it reaches a sane number. Add memestock fuckery in and it just proves that anyone who is touching this stock at the moment other than to hold deserves to lose their money.

62401771
>>62397993

If you think this is low just wait until the 6mo-1y sell off from internals.

62401805
>>62397993

internal pre‑IPO share price for SpaceX was** $421 per share. this is the last confirmed internal transaction price before the company went public. This figure comes directly from SpaceX’s December2025 internal tender offer, where the company approved an internal share sale valuing SpaceX at roughly $800billion, explicitly pricing internal stock at $421 per share.

BREH OH MEIN GOT

62401818
>>62400173
THIS! all of the videos of Elon's rockets are AI generated of course

62401864
>>62397993
elon sends his regards and thanks you for falling for yet another of his grifts

62401908
>>62397993
Hold and claim "it was always generational hodl"

62402110
>>62401805
>internal pre‑IPO share price for SpaceX was** $421 per share
KEK. Imagine being the retard who bought at that price

62402134
>>62398373
>our company is worth 28 trillion dollars
>out of this 2 trillion is the actual company doing stuff
>the other 26 trillion is that grok will take us to alpha centaury and create a galactic empire in 2 more weeks
this will never stop being funny

62402144
>>62402110

Musk was the one who purchased internally at that price at 421$ per share.

He spent est 1.5bn.

The comparison here is actually pretty wild if you look at it in the context of Bezos/Zucc,(who both did the same thing)

62402173
Oh, so looking at the time release tranch by the way, the positive performance idea in regards to it is around 175 expectation at Q2 earnings in an optimistic posture, so now is still time to buy. 10% additional stock will be released in those terms, so at Q2 earnings is the time to sell if you're looking at an early max exit.

If you bought in at the very initial 135 the best time to sell would be at the Q2 earnings for easy gains. Then you rebuy at 180/366.

Best buys are at Q2 earnings too probably.

62402176
>>62397993
ELON MASK DO NOT REDEEM THE STOCK SAAR

62402205
>>62397993
>He didn't even try to pump it
>IPO 135
>Current price 160
Halp, I'm confused at these retards

62402232
>>62402173

That's three dump opportunities, three sell opportunities, and three buy opportunities. (Micro-opportunities at every time released tranch, as well.)
Q2 earnings, employee liquidity opening, then institutional at 366.

62402238
>>62402232

180 days/366 days that is.

62402312
Every tranch will -reduce- price unless there are still those who desire it.

So if they don't meet Q-2 earnings target at 176$ that's it, it's over. Each successive tranch will dilute share value by 3-5% (at minimum) after that until 180 days /366 days.

So if Q2 earnings failure you would see what, around half the valuation optimistically?


>Drops to under 75$ if Q2 target isn't met organically a year from now.


Though, there are strong loyalists at Musk Companies, the culture is very strong. It is likely that there won't be sell offs unless morale erodes significantly and there is a transition to seeing it as an exit. These could be bagholders too.

62402398
>>62398483
kek baggie

62402435
>>62397993
elon cuck will cuck u all up nigger

62402515
>>62398726
The technology is amazing, nobody disputes that
But it doesnt translate to a profitable business yet, nor will it ever translate to a £2-3 trillion company
there is no $2-3 trillion market where this amazing technology fits

62403719
>>62401805
>>62402110
>>62402144
this is $421 pre-5:1 split, so $84.20 per share compared to the SPCX shared price.

62403739
>>62403719
>this is $421 pre-5:1 split
>split
Ahh, I see.

>$84.20 per share
Kek. So it still has room to fall, I guess?

62403974
>>62403739
this was the last internal tender offer to insiders, but the last pre-IPO valuation had the share price at $526

62404050
>>62397993
You were literally warned about the SpaceX IPO SCAM constantly. Why didn't you listen?

62404125
Elon will get trump to include spacex in the S&P500 so boomers and millenials can pump up the stock with their pensions

62404293
this is just the local stock market drop, if spcx doesn't go back to near 135, it will recover by friday and the reddit fags can go back to seething at elon for being a trillionaire while they wage away their lives into oblivion

62404295
SpaceX is already valued so high. Not sure why people think its going to 500x or whatever.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 105 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-06-24 03:20 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

62389340
I feel like something new has to happen before the market shifts sentiment.

All the ETFs and government regulation just hurt the project.
Nobody is utilizing the tools, the tools still feel beta, and developers are being arrested.

If it's just going to be a big gay digital rock the momentum grinds to a halt.

The problem is some of us got rich and have something to lose now.
Different factions formed inside the developer community.
Is there incentive to free ball the network still? idk.

I'm still holding but this bear feels different

62390164
>>62388819
So how will you change it?

62391118
>>62389340
I'm still holding as well.

62391156
>>62371718
It feels like shit when it goes down but when you're near the bottom like now you just remember the last times you felt this way and how it gongo up from here, everytime. Bobos are ngmi. Mumu supremacy is real. Cope trannies.

62391165
>>62383619
Based retard

62391192
>>62371718
The 20% long-term-capital-gains tax rate that I'd have to pay if I cashed out all at once.

62391471
The only investments I hold are schizo nonsense Chainlink and PMs. I have a good amount of money through my Roth and 401k. I can only invest in specific etfs through my work 401k. What’s the best method to get long term Bitcoin exposure through a IRA?

62392524
>>62391156
You're not a real investor

62393205
>>62372380
Then how come I can send BTC to my Bitcoin core wallet dumbfuck?

62393451
>>62371718
I believe in the philosophy that led to bitcoin. Sadly the vast majority of humanity are just cattle. I still hold because I believe there is something that can happen. The pessimism on this board doesn't change my view on this. I will hold and if it fails I'll be ok because I have stocks and silver and land.

62395366
>>62391192
That's too much to be honest

62395566
>>62371718
I plan on selling when I retire in ten years. But who knows maybe I will change my mind and keep holding.

62396187
All these retards are greedy, that's all there is to it

62398052
>>62395566
Keep going anon

62399672
>>62396187
Oh really huh?

62399688
>>62371718
I forgot the passwords for one account and everything got stolen from the other.

62401169
>>62399688
Story maker

62403086
>>62393205
I guess you know the answer

62403127
>>62371718
If you all feel fucked, just remember some farmer in Australia sold all his life saving and put $10 million onto s&p6900.
I bet that cunt has necked himself now.

62403902
>>62371718
my conviction comes from relentless money printing

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 145 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 145 • Updated: 2026-06-23 20:18 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398213
>>62381795
wtf is mann._michael13 on about?

62398226
bros im worried OP will tie the noose after getting pumped and dumped, we need to be much more reassuring to our friends that mistakes happen and we can overcome mistakes if we work together. Don't let op be another hanged man in the bathtub meme.

62398877
>>62392029
I hate the French

62398970
>>62398090
This animu was a fun watch. Hope you enjoyed it too schadenfreude bro

62398978
>>62375128
>2pbtid

62399080
>>62375150
Bet.

Guess my gains.

62399090
>>62375128
You were saying?

62399160
>>62375140
Well, think of it like this. The right half is all the economic activity that having a jetliner enabled for humanity. Space is vaster still than the air. Space can do what air can do, and more, we can get resources in space. So you have to imagine that the total economic potential of space can be as great if not greater than the economic activity from air travel.
Spacex is the closest of all companies to making it so that the biggest cost to sending something to space is just the fuel to do it. Can they achieve that? If so, then they have a the jetliner of space travel. It would take tens if not hundreds of billions, and at least a decade if not 2 or 3 to replicate what spacex has done, from scratch. Most of their competitors aren't starting from scratch, but they are way behind. If you wanted it done quickly, it would be very expensive, and perhaps not even possible to buy the capability that spacex presents itself capable of delivering.

62399168
>>62375128
The one cow village of OP.

62399196
>>62375128
It is absolutely uncanny how /biz/ calls the top like this.
Anons like OP are like a inverse oracles.

62399203
>>62399196
not really it was kinda obvious
its priced like a top 10 company but makes no money. Even it does start to make top 10 company profit the price wont go anywhere so yeah

62399214
Rip to all the jeets falling for elon scam. I guess your villages are going to starve in the next few months

62399292
>>62399203
Yeah you could expect spacex to go down, but the timing is always tied to gloat posting.
>OH NO NO NO
>YOU’RE SO DUMB FOR BEING BEARISH
>HA HA HA HA
You could almost set puts to this.

62399317
SPCX will drop to at least the IPO price in the near term. I wouldn't even consider buying it until that happens.

62399364
>>62375784
>You could have 10x or 100x real estate
>but you don't want to make it up into outer space

62399387
>>62375128
The absolute STATE of OP...

>>62398185
What the fuck is the purpose of these bots? Why is this.shot.literally in every single thread in this board? How come jannies and mods do nothing about this spam?

62399575
>>62399160
Your first mistake was assuming spacex is a space company.

62400470
this is unironically going below 50. Retards who bought this and were shilling it here for a month finally got what they deserved. Even my fucking bank was shilling this scam to me. Remember it's better to miss out on too many oppotunities than buy too many scams

62400488
>>62375163
I love how lefties constantly do shit that nerfs their prosperity. ESG, DEI, Elon bad etc etc

62400495
>>62400488
hello did you look at the chart recently?

62400827
>>62398185
Owari da

62400854
>>62375140
>>62375163
Haha

62401251
>>62375393
What exactly has he delivered?

62401264
>>62375393
you're stupid as fuck, spacex stock price has literally nothing to do with space

62401317
>>62400470
In my third world shithole the rightoid media (owners are musk allies) was shilling it on TV last friday
>not into trading stocks? this might be the perfect time to start, the world's first trillionaire bla bla bla
As if they would ever want plebs to get rich
Just an obvious pump and dump

62401616
>>62400470
i left a buy order for $160 by accident, my stock trading company has a thing where they cancel any buy order if its not fulfilled after like a week, so i forgot about it. It crashed today below 160 and i bought it when i didn't intend to. Oops, 5k pissed away on this overpriced stock lmao.

62401764
>>62401616
>i left a buy order for $160 by accident
that's not an accident anon. You fell for the shilling.

>>62401317
yes basically a meme coin. Could see it from miles away. IN doubt, always remember, better to buy less things than too many

62401801
>>62375809

Have sex, have kids, enjoy your life, continue to fantasize about young romance, invest, play vidya games, AND post on 4chan.

62402502
>>62401801

62402710
>>62375128
